_mapping_meta.scope: Revision of M10-A (274V) and M10-B (339V) only. Other sub-groups unchanged.
_mapping_meta.note: Term-level routing rules with per-verse exceptions. CC resolves to flat vc_id assignments. All 613 M10-A + M10-B verses must be re-routed to one of the 16 new sub-groups.

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Wilful sinning
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R1 — The will committing a known wrong: sinning as a deliberate relational act where the person knows what they are doing and does it

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Unintentional sinning
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R2 — Sin committed without deliberate intent; real and morally consequential; once surfacing to awareness, conscience engaged and remedy required

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Confession
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R3 — The moment the inner person names a committed sin as sin: conscience in confessional mode, the will owning what was done

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Conscience suppression
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R4 — The inner person who does not recognize, denies, or suppresses awareness of their own sinful state; conscience absent, self-blinded, or falsely claiming innocence

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Refusal to repent
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R5 — The will that persistently refuses to turn from a sinful course when departure is available and known to be required; turning actively withheld

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Habitual defection
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R6 — Sin as a recurring, established pattern of the will over time; the will's settled direction toward sin

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Contagious sin
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R7 — Sin as a moral direction transmitted through authority: a leader's inner commitment to wrongdoing bends the will of those under their influence

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Political revolt
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R8 — Breaking submission to established authority; wilful political defection carrying moral weight of sinful transgression

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Sinful speech
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R9 — Sin committed through the mouth: blasphemy and slander originating in inner moral corruption, directed against God or persons

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Specialised sinful mechanisms
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R10 — Sin operating through specific inner mechanisms: desire exploited by enticement, identity falsified through hypocrisy, covenantal identity abandoned through apostasy

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Sin as universal condition
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R11 — Sin as a structural condition of the inner person: every person is under sin's dominion, fallen short of humanity's intended end

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Sin as enslaving power
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R12 — Sin as an active force that masters the inner person, governing the will so that the person cannot freely choose otherwise

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Sin as divine record
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R13 — Sin as an objective moral weight existing before God: recorded, accumulated, not hidden from divine memory; the inner person carries this record whether they feel it or not

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Forgiveness sought and received
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R14 — The inner movement toward God in search of forgiveness; the will that turns and appeals for sin's removal; the divine response of granting or declaring pardon

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Generational sin
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R15 — Sin as a moral condition that enters through lineage: inherited from ancestors, transmitted through family or national belonging

new_subgroup_definitions.label: Wilful persistence under confrontation
new_subgroup_definitions.characteristic: Char-R16 — The inner state of refusing to release sin even while its wrongness is known and confronted; the obdurate will, entrenched under challenge

split_rules.rule: Conscience suppression / self-deception: Jer 2:35; Jer 16:10; 1Jo 1:10; Ecc 8:12; Ecc 2:26; Job 35:6; Judg 20:16 (literal)
Habitual pattern: Hos 8:11; Hos 10:9; Ecc 7:20; Ecc 7:26; 1Sa 2:25; 2Ki 17:7; Exo 9:34; 1Ki 8:46; Ecc 9:2; Ecc 9:18; Pro 11:31; Pro 13:22
Divine record / burden: Exo 32:33; Num 32:23; Lam 1:8; Lam 5:16; Psa 51:7; Pro 20:2; Hab 2:10; Zep 1:17; Job 8:4; Job 10:14; Job 24:19
Generational inheritance: Isa 43:27; Lam 5:7 (primary X); Jer 14:20; Isa 65:20; 1Ki 8:47; Psa 78:17; Psa 78:32; Isa 64:5; Jer 8:14; Eze 37:23

term_routing_rules.note: All remaining cha.ta verses not matching a split rule route to M10-J (wilful sinning). CC resolves per-verse using reference list above.

Conscience suppression: 1Cor 15:34; Tit 3:11
Refusal / persistence: 1Ti 5:20; Heb 10:26; Rom 6:15

Enslaving power / liberation: 1Jo 3:8; 1Jo 3:9; 1Jo 5:18; Eph 4:26; 1Jo 5:16
Interpersonal / deliberate: Mat 18:15; Mat 18:21; Luk 17:3; Luk 17:4; 1Cor 8:12; Joh 5:14; Joh 8:11; Act 25:8; 1Pe 2:20; 2Pe 2:4; Joh 9:2; Joh 9:3

split_rules.rule: Burden / record requiring removal: Joh 1:29; 1Pe 2:24; Heb 9:26/28; Rev 1:5; Isa 53:12 (if present)
Forgiveness / removal received: Mat 26:28; Luk 7:47/48; Act 2:38; Act 10:43; 1Co 15:3; Col 1:14; Eph 1:7; 1Jo 1:7; 1Jo 2:12; Heb 10:12; Heb 10:17/18
Confession / naming: where hamartia verse is primarily a confession context (rare — most confession uses hamartanō verb)
Wilful act register: Heb 12:1 (sin entangling — deliberate clinging)
Atonement / forgiveness sought and mediated: Lev 4:3/14/26/28/35; Lev 5:6/10/13; Lev 10:17/19; Lev 16:16/30/34; Lev 19:22; Exo 32:30
Refusal / persistence: 1Ki 16:31; all 'did not turn from sin' chat.tat patterns
Generational: Lev 26:39; Num 14:18
Leadership transmitted: defection-transmitted patterns in chat.tat
Universal condition: chat.tat framing sin as shared human condition (Gen 4:7 predatory-force dimension → M10-U if enslaving; → M10-V if burden)
Forgiveness / justification: Eph 1:7; Col 2:13 (forgiveness framing); 2Cor 5:19; Mat 6:14/15
Act register: Gal 6:1 (caught in a trespass — specific act)
